title: Database Migrations
summary: Alembic is the database migration tool used with SQLAlchemy. Migrations are versioned scripts that evolve the database schema over time — adding columns, creating tables, dropping indexes — without losing existing data. Each service manages its own migrations independently.
keyPoints: alembic upgrade head: applies all pending migrations to bring the DB to the latest version
alembic downgrade -1: reverts the most recent migration
alembic revision --autogenerate: generates a new migration file from ORM model changes
Migration files live in alembic/versions/ and are committed to version control
Each migration has an up_revision and a down_revision forming a chain
Migrations must be run on first deploy and after any schema change
Never edit a migration that has already been applied to a production database

exercises.prompt: What does running 'alembic upgrade head' do in a GameHub service?
options: Applies all pending database migrations up to the latest version
Generates a new SQLAlchemy model file
exercises.answer: Applies all pending database migrations up to the latest version
exercises.explanation: 'head' refers to the latest migration in the version chain. All unapplied migrations between the current DB state and head are executed in order.

exercises.prompt: Complete the Alembic command to auto-generate a new migration file after adding a column to an ORM model.
exercises.explanation: --autogenerate compares your ORM models to the current database schema and generates the diff as a migration script.

exercises.prompt: You need to undo the last migration in development. Which command do you run?
exercises.explanation: downgrade moves the schema back; -1 means one step back from the current version. You can also target a specific revision ID.
